issues: Headline is AI-invented and does not reflect any actual headline in the OCR — no headline is visible in the OCR text.

The draft omits key individual contributors mentioned in the article: Matt Hervey (hat trick, including the game-winner), Dean Rutledge, and Brian Kozak scored the three power-play goals that sealed the win.
The draft does not mention that the power play resulted from Rustler defenceman Keith Lightbown ejecting for hitting Dave Tomlinson (who never dropped his gloves) — a key narrative detail.
The draft describes Kurtenbach only as 'Coach' but the OCR identifies him as 'general manager-coach'.
The short description says 'Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal' which is accurate, but omits that this was Game 2 of the series (the opener was an 11-4 Richmond win).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Red Deer Rustlers 6-4 in Game 2 of the Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal, extending their post-season winning streak to 17 games and taking a 2-0 series lead.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes rallied from a 4-3 deficit with 13 minutes remaining in Game 2 of their Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal against the Red Deer Rustlers on April 8, 1987, at Richmond Arena, winning 6-4 to extend a post-season winning streak to 17 consecutive games. Richmond's comeback was sparked by a five-minute power play after Rustler defenceman Keith Lightbown was ejected for attacking Sockeye forward Dave Tomlinson, who never dropped his gloves; Dean Rutledge tied the game, Matt Hervey scored the winner (his hat-trick goal), and Brian Kozak added insurance during the man advantage. Despite strong goaltending from Red Deer pick-up Ken Weiss and a disciplined Rustler defence that had frustrated the Sockeyes through two periods, general manager-coach Orland Kurtenbach's club pulled out the victory to lead the best-of-seven series 2-0, with all remaining games to be played in Red Deer.
review.notes: No headline is discernible in the OCR; the AI-generated headline should be removed. Article is a game report filed from Richmond, B.C., appearing in the Red Deer Advocate on April 9, 1987. No byline is present in the OCR.
